description: State-dependent noise: sigma_crisis = boost * sigma_calm when |d_i| > stress_thr=0.0015
key_finding: The calibrated model already produces extreme excess kurtosis (74.5 above Gaussian) from circuit-breaker clipping and shock nonlinearities — well above typical empirical estimates of 4-10. State-dependent noise (sigma_crisis > sigma_calm) reduces simulated kurtosis while preserving all 4 calibrated moments: boost=2.0 yields excess kurtosis 37.9, boost=3.0 yields 13.5. This provides a calibration lever that narrows the 4th-moment gap without disturbing the other calibrated moments.
paper_statement: The baseline model produces large excess kurtosis (~75) relative to empirical stablecoin 1-min returns (~4-10), driven by the circuit-breaker threshold and shock injection. A state-dependent noise extension (sigma_crisis = 2-3 × sigma_calm) reduces simulated kurtosis to 13-41 while preserving all four calibrated moments (4/4 at every boost level). Full kurtosis matching is deferred to future work requiring finer shock-distribution calibration.
